response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly accomplished Staff/Senior engineer with 15+ years of experience, founding-level work, conference talks, and open-source contributions — all of which make him severely overqualified for an entry-level individual contributor role. While his TypeScript, React, and AWS skills technically satisfy the requirements, hiring him into a junior/mid position creates an almost certain mismatch in salary expectations, day-to-day challenge level, and retention likelihood. The role explicitly targets candidates with first-to-several years of experience; he has over a decade and a half across embedded systems, high-throughput microservices, agentic AI infrastructure, and platform founding.
strengths: Deep TypeScript and Node.js proficiency demonstrated across multiple senior roles (Optilyz, re:cinq), directly matching the stack required.
Strong software craftsmanship ethos — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, clean architecture — aligns with the role's architectural interests.
Covers multiple nice-to-have qualifications: LLM/MCP agentic tooling, AWS CDK, and Kubernetes deployment experience.
concerns: Massively overqualified — current title is Staff Software Engineer; this is an entry-level IC role, creating near-certain retention and engagement risk.
No explicit PostgreSQL experience; primary database exposure is MongoDB and MySQL, with SQL listed generically.
Compensation expectations from a 15+ year Staff-level engineer will almost certainly exceed the budget of a junior/mid role.
